by Section 63 of the Indian Succession Act. As in the 
case of proof of other documents so in the case of proof 
of wills it would be idle to expect proof with mathematical 
certainty. The test to be applied would be the usual test 
of the satisfaction of the prudent mind in such matters.”
26. Further, in the case of Meena Pradhan and Others v. Kamla Pradhan 
and Another9 following essentials to prove a Will were mentioned:
“10.1. The court has to consider two aspects : firstly, that 
the will is executed by the testator, and secondly, that it 
was the last will executed by him;
10.2. It is not required to be proved with mathematical 
accuracy, but the test of satisfaction of the prudent mind 
has to be applied.
10.3. A will is required to fulfil all the formalities required 
under Section 63 of the Succession Act, that is to say:
